title: Result Recording
learning_objectives: Understand Result Recording in SAP QM.
Learn how inspection results are recorded.
Learn important SAP Result Recording transaction codes.
Understand how Result Recording affects the inspection process.

introduction.title: What is Result Recording?
introduction.content: Result Recording is the process of entering inspection results into SAP after the quality inspection has been performed. The recorded results are compared with predefined specifications in the Inspection Plan to determine whether the inspected material meets quality standards.

business_purpose.title: Why is Result Recording Important?
business_purpose.content: Result Recording provides objective evidence of product quality. It ensures inspection results are documented, traceable and available for quality analysis, audits and decision-making.

inspection_characteristics.description: Measured using numerical values such as thickness, width, weight or hardness.
Recorded as observations such as Pass/Fail, Accept/Reject or Visual OK/Not OK.

step_by_step_procedure: Enter measured values or inspection results.
Record defects or remarks if required.
system_updates: Inspection results are stored in SAP.
Results become available for Usage Decision.
Inspection history is maintained for future analysis.

integration.purpose: Uses recorded inspection results to determine acceptance or rejection.
Quality results are linked with production output.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: After manufacturing a stainless steel coil, the inspector measures thickness (5.02 mm), width (1250 mm), hardness (195 HB) and visually checks the surface finish. These values are entered in QE51N. SAP compares them with the acceptance limits defined in the Inspection Plan before the material proceeds to the Usage Decision.

best_practices: Enter inspection results immediately after inspection.
common_errors: Recording results for the wrong Inspection Lot.
important_notes: Inspection results should reflect actual measured values.
Every mandatory characteristic must be recorded before making the Usage Decision.
Inspection records provide traceability during quality audits.
jsl_notes: Inspectors should follow the approved JSL inspection standards while recording results.
All mandatory inspection characteristics must be completed before forwarding the Inspection Lot for Usage Decision.
Any non-conformance should be documented according to plant procedures.
